concept_ref: §3 row 1 — '100% statement coverage = fully tested' / §4 Level 1 narrative

scenario.title: The Missing Else
scenario.narrative: A pricing function applies a discount only to premium users. The 'happy path' test was added; the team claims testing is complete.

claim: T1 below achieves 100% Statement coverage, so the function is fully tested.
hints: The first half of the claim is true; the second half is the trap. Re-read Law Card 1's Limitation field.
Statement coverage only asks 'did each line execute?' — but what about the implicit FALSE branch of the if? Is there a test where is_premium=False?
Solution — Statement coverage IS 100% (every line executed via T1), but Branch coverage is 0% on the false edge. 'Fully tested' is a misconception (Concept Analysis §3 row 1). Verdict: claim INVALID.

seeded_faults.description: Hidden bug on the implicit else path: a non-premium user is double-charged due to a missing reset.

misconceptions.explanation_md: 100% Statement coverage does NOT mean 'fully tested'. An `if` without an `else` creates a bypass path that Statement coverage cannot see. You must add a test where the condition is FALSE to exercise the implicit branch (Branch testing, Law Card 2).
